formations_blocks: Paraguay dropping deep into a defensive block when Australia have possession
Transitional, unsettled early phase with both sides trying to establish physicality
adjustments: Metcalfe pushing up on the left for Australia
Volpato operating on the right for Australia
Bos starting on the right in defence with Behich on the left
Australia recycling possession side to side to probe for gaps
Paraguay retreating quickly behind the ball after losing possession
phases.summary: Australia begin in a 5-2-3 with Metcalfe and Volpato advanced wide, trying to work the ball patiently against a Paraguay side sitting in a compact 5-3-2 and happy to drop deep. Early passages are physical and transitional, with Australia creating the better openings through crisp combination play, Volpato’s running, and a fierce Irvine effort, while Paraguay mostly defend the box and look to avoid defeat.

formations_blocks: both sides set up to counterattack
midfield transitions with early forward passing rather than sustained possession
adjustments: Australia concentrating attacks down the right through Volpato and Bos overlapping
Paraguay using theatrics/fouls to disrupt rhythm and slow play
key_matchups: Diego Gomez vs Herrington in the air
Volpato and Bos combining on Australia’s right flank
phases.summary: The first-half spell was played at a brisk tempo because both teams were prioritising counterattacks and moving the ball forward quickly whenever midfield duels were won. Australia had the clearer tactical edge, especially through repeated right-sided combinations between Volpato and Bos, but their delivery from wide areas and set pieces repeatedly let them down. Paraguay were forced into more reactive defending, with some physical duels and time-wasting tactics used to break Australia’s momentum.

formations_blocks: Australia with an extra man in midfield
adjustments: Paraguay shifted from a back four to a back five
Australia finding joy through Volpato on the right
'More of the same' suggested for the second half
key_matchups: Volpato on the right against Paraguay's reshaped defensive line
phases.summary: At half-time the key tactical point is Paraguay’s switch from a back four to a back five, a move that has given Australia an extra midfield man. Paul Okon noted that this has allowed Volpato to find space on the right, and Australia want to continue in the same way after the break. The goalless scoreline also suits both sides given the group standings, so the second half begins with little immediate pressure to force the issue.

formations_blocks: more assertive Paraguay early in the second half
midfield circulation through a metronome to move Australia side to side
play concentrated either side of halfway with transition moments
adjustments: Paraguay made a halftime substitution: Mauricio replaced Maidana
Mauricio brought on to help Paraguay circulate the ball in midfield and act as the metronome
Australia were warned to guard against transitions as Paraguay looked more dangerous after the break
phases.summary: Paraguay emerged from the break with more energy and confidence, using Mauricio’s introduction to stabilise midfield circulation and make Australia defend from side to side. Their greater intent also increased transition threat, with Enciso beginning to trouble the Australian back line as Australia’s defending and decision-making under pressure became less convincing.

formations_blocks: both defences dropping deep out of possession
adjustments: professional fouls increasing to disrupt rhythm
Australia invite Paraguay to stand off and keep the ball moving on the right
Australia substitutions: Yengi and Okon-Engstler on for Irvine and Irankunda
Australia settle into a clear 5-4-1 after the changes
key_matchups: Irankunda vs two and three defenders
phases.summary: The closing phase became a tense grind, with both teams dropping deep, slowing the game through fouls and protecting space as the risk of conceding rose. Australia moved into a clear 5-4-1 and were content with the 0-0, while Paraguay relied on Enciso and isolated moments to threaten, but neither side could land a decisive blow.
